description: Cleaning, normalization, deduplication and conflict-resolution steps applied to the merged PDF (Practice 3) + web/dataset (Practice 4) extraction tables to produce the publication-ready dataset at data/processed/dataset.csv.

steps.description: From each extracted CSV keep only the columns listed in specs/dataset_schema.json. Drop helper columns (page, table_id, evidence_text, review_status, zenodo_row_index, smiles_source, monomer_a_smiles, monomer_b_smiles, etc.). Missing schema columns are added as empty.
Concatenate the column-aligned PDF and web tables into one interim table.
Trim whitespace, collapse runs of whitespace, normalize Unicode to NFC for all string fields. Replace common missing-value tokens (NA, N/A, -, none, null, '') with empty strings.

Map polymer_class values from each source to the closed vocabulary defined in dataset_schema.json. The Zenodo dataset uses plural English labels (Polysiloxanes, Polyvinyls, ...), the PDF extraction uses snake_case (polyimide, polyphenylene_oxide). Both are mapped through scripts/clean_dataset.py POLYMER_CLASS_MAP. Anything that does not resolve is set to 'other' and the original label appended to notes.
Standardize the tg_unit field. Variants like 'degC', '°C', 'Celsius', 'celsius' -> 'C'. Variants like 'Kelvin', 'kelvin', 'k' -> 'K'. Rows with any other value fail this step and are dropped.
Cast tg_value and tg_std to float. Rows where tg_value cannot be parsed or is null are dropped (the schema marks tg_value as required). Dropped rows are logged with reason='tg_value_missing'.

Internally convert (tg_value, tg_unit) to Kelvin and reject rows outside the physically reasonable polymer Tg range [100, 900] K. The original tg_value and tg_unit are preserved in the published dataset; the conversion is only used for the range check. Dropped rows logged with reason='tg_out_of_range'.

repeat_unit_smiles is required by the schema. Rows with an empty SMILES (typical for polyimides whose repeat unit was not auto-assembled in Practice 3) are dropped from the published dataset and logged with reason='smiles_missing'. They remain in data/extracted/ for future enrichment.

Map measurement_method to the schema vocabulary: DSC, DMA, TMA, dilatometry, dielectric_spectroscopy, simulation_MD, unknown. Anything not recognized becomes 'unknown' with the original value appended to notes.
Every source_id must appear in specs/source_map.json. Rows pointing to undeclared sources are dropped and logged with reason='unknown_source_id'.
Drop exact duplicates (all schema columns equal). This is a low-aggressive deduplication that only removes accidental copies.

Within rows that share the same repeat_unit_smiles, if Tg values disagree by more than 20 K when converted to Kelvin, append 'cross_source_disagreement' to the notes of all affected rows. No row is dropped: the dataset deliberately retains multiple measurements because Tg depends on heating rate, method, molecular weight and tacticity, and downstream modelers should see the spread.
Verify that the resulting dataframe has exactly the columns declared in specs/dataset_schema.json, in the same order, and that all required fields are populated. Failure aborts the pipeline.
Write data/processed/dataset.csv with UTF-8 encoding, comma-separated, no index, schema-aligned column order.

design_decisions: tg_value and tg_unit are preserved as reported. A normalized Kelvin column is intentionally NOT added because the schema does not declare one; downstream consumers can compute it deterministically.

Records with missing tg_value or missing repeat_unit_smiles are removed from the published dataset because the schema marks both as required. They remain available in data/extracted/ and are listed in data/interim/cleaning_drops.csv with the reason.

Conflicting Tg values across sources for the same repeat unit are FLAGGED but NOT averaged or removed: Tg legitimately varies with method, heating rate, molecular weight, and tacticity. Downstream users should choose their own aggregation policy.
